text:
GAHIZ
جاهز
STAY GAHIZ


Master Project Knowledge File  |  Version 3.0  |  May 2026

PART 1: BRAND IDENTITY


1.1 Core Identity
Product Name
Gahiz (جاهز) — means 'Ready' in Egyptian Arabic


Slogan
Stay Gahiz — Stay Ready


Vision
Standardize the Egyptian BPO workforce, transforming high-intent youth into global assets


Mission
Egypt's first Ethical, AI-Driven BPO Pipeline — eliminating the ban cycle with precision screening and AI-powered recovery training


Tagline (Arabic)
ابقَ جاهز — Stay Gahiz


1.2 Brand Voice
Tone: Smart Egyptian friend. Direct. Zero corporate language.
Language: Egyptian colloquial Arabic for candidates. English for investors/admin.
Score labels: Always in English — Clarity, Confidence, Fluency, Energy, Relevance
Price: Always in EGP. Never USD.
Attitude: Honest, tough but fair — like a mentor who has been on both sides

1.3 Colors & Typography
Primary: #DC143C (Crimson Red) — energy, urgency, excellence
Secondary: #0F172A (Deep Navy) — stability, professionalism, trust
Accent: #06B6D4 (Cyan) — clarity, insight, breakthrough
Neutral: #F5F5F5 (Off-White) — backgrounds, cards
Typography: Cairo Bold (Arabic), Inter Medium (English)

1.4 What Gahiz Is / Is Not
Gahiz IS:
An interview stamina simulation platform
A confidence builder under pressure
A reality checker with honest AI feedback
Egypt's first BPO-specific interview prep product

Gahiz IS NOT:
An English tutoring service
A motivational speaker platform
A corporate training firm
A generic interview prep app




PART 2: PRODUCT ARCHITECTURE


2.1 The Core Concept: Stamina Challenge
The product is built around one idea: can the candidate hold their stamina under increasing pressure? Not just 'can you speak English' — but 'can you maintain fluency, energy, and clarity as the pressure mounts?' This mirrors exactly what real BPO recruiters test.

2.2 The 4-Level Real Hiring Journey
This is what candidates actually face at Teleperformance, Vodafone, Concentrix, Amazon BPO Egypt:

Level 1: WhatsApp Voice Note (2 minutes)
Candidate records a 2-minute voice message about themselves
Sends directly to recruiter (or via recruiter link)
Recruiter listens and decides: Yes or No
FIRST filter. Most candidates fail here.

Level 2: Phone Interview (10–20 minutes)
If approved from Level 1
Recruiter calls candidate directly
Asks live interview questions in real-time
Tests: composure under pressure, clarity, relevance of answers

Level 3: Virtual AI Interview (7 questions x 4 minutes each)
If passed Level 2
Candidate receives AI interview link from the company
Must record 7 video answers (4 minutes each)
Submitted and reviewed by AI scoring system
Companies using: HireVue, Talview, Spark Hire

Level 4: Final HR Interview
If passed Level 3
Direct interview with company HR manager
Either job offer or rejection

2.3 The 3-Stage Stamina Challenge (Free Funnel)
Gahiz simulates the real hiring journey through 3 progressively harder stages — all free — designed to expose the candidate's stamina collapse point and convert them to paid.

Stage 1: The Hero (60 seconds — Free)
Candidate records 60-second WhatsApp voice note about themselves
Same format as what they send to a real recruiter
AI analyzes immediately
Target score: 90%+ (B2+) = SUCCESS
Sample AI Report: "Solid energy! You said I HAVE 24 YEARS instead of I AM 24. Status: Ready for Stage 2"

Stage 2: The Warning (120 seconds — Free)
Candidate records 2-minute chaos de-escalation scenario
Tests: Logic, Flow, Stamina under emotional pressure
Target: 75%+ (B1+)
Sample AI Report: "Polite but repetitive. Used PROBLEM 4 times. Use ISSUE or CONCERN. Status: Warning — Fluency is dipping"

Stage 3: The Wall (240 seconds — Free)
4-minute marathon — the hardest stage
This is where most candidates collapse
Measures: Total system collapse point, filler words, accent flag
Sample AI Report: "Total collapse at 2:15. Filler words: 300% (22 units). MTI Accent Flag: 48%. Status: UN-HIRABLE. Zero stamina."

2.4 Moment of Truth (Conversion Point)
After Stage 3, the candidate sees exactly where and why they collapsed. Two paths appear:

Option A: Retrospection (Free)
Review the fail data
One more 4-minute simulation free
No coaching. No improvement tools. Zero stamina training.

Option B: Restoration — 200 EGP (CONVERSION)
7-day Endurance Gym access
12 nightmare scenario simulations
Unlimited practice sessions
Real-time score tracking
Mirrors real AI interview interfaces from actual companies

2.5 Pricing Structure
Free Tier
3-Stage Stamina Challenge via WhatsApp. No payment required.


200 EGP / 7 Days
12 nightmare scenario simulations + unlimited practice + real-time stamina tracking


⚠ Coached sessions (1-on-1 with Ahmed) are Phase 3 only. Do not mention them in Phase 1 marketing or website copy.

2.6 Salary Context (Market Reality 2026)
Entry-level call center Egypt 2026: 15,000–18,000 EGP/month
Mid-level (6–12 months): 18,000–22,000 EGP/month
Senior/Team Lead: 25,000+ EGP/month
This is 3–4x minimum wage — real financial independence for the target candidate
200 EGP = less than 1% of monthly salary at entry level — extremely low barrier




PART 3: AI DIAGNOSTIC ENGINE (THE MIRROR)


3.1 How The Mirror Works
BPO Recruiter AI = 1st calibration standard
Gahiz Engine mirrors exact recruiter scoring criteria
Mapped to linguistic decay algorithms from: Concentrix, Vodafone UK, Teleperformance
Validates vocabulary flags (overused words, filler patterns)
Identifies Mother Tongue Influence (MTI) — Arabic accent detection

3.2 Scoring Dimensions
Clarity
Is speech clear and understandable? Score: 1–10


Confidence
Does the voice sound confident? Score: 1–10


Relevance
Does the answer address the question? Score: 1–10


Fluency
Is English smooth and natural? Score: 1–10


Energy
Is there enthusiasm in the voice? Score: 1–10


3.3 Verdict Thresholds
PASS
Average score >= 7.5 across all dimensions


BORDERLINE
Average score 5.5 – 7.4


UN-HIRABLE
Average score < 5.5 OR stamina collapse detected before 2:00 in Stage 3


3.4 Stamina Collapse Metrics
Total collapse timestamp (when fluency drops to zero)
Sentence complexity score (target: 48%+)
Filler word count and percentage (target: <10 units)
MTI Accent Flag percentage (Arabic accent on specific phonemes)
Problem word frequency (same word used 3+ times = flag)

3.5 The OpenAI Scoring System Prompt
This is the exact prompt sent to OpenAI GPT-4 to generate the candidate report:

SYSTEM PROMPT — GAHIZ AI SCORING ENGINE

You are the Gahiz AI Diagnostic Engine. Your job is to score a BPO/call center interview candidate based on their transcribed voice answer.

CONTEXT:
- This candidate is Egyptian, applying to international call center jobs (Teleperformance, Vodafone UK, Concentrix, Amazon BPO Egypt)
- They are being judged by international BPO recruiters who score against B2+ English fluency
- Most candidates fail because of stamina collapse, filler words, MTI accent, and low energy — not because of grammar errors

SCORE THESE 5 DIMENSIONS (1-10 each):
1. Clarity — Is the speech clear and understandable to a native English speaker?
2. Confidence — Does the voice sound confident, or hesitant and apologetic?
3. Relevance — Does the answer actually address the question asked?
4. Fluency — Is the English smooth and natural? Note: filler words, pauses, restarts
5. Energy — Is there enthusiasm and drive? Would a recruiter be engaged?

VERDICT THRESHOLDS:
PASS = Average >= 7.5 | BORDERLINE = 5.5-7.4 | FAIL = < 5.5

REPORT TONE:
Write in bilingual Egyptian style (Arabic + English mix). Be direct, honest, and actionable. Write like a tough but fair mentor, NOT like a corporate HR system. Use Egyptian colloquial Arabic for the narrative sections. Keep score labels in English. Never be vague — always cite a specific example from the transcript.

OUTPUT FORMAT (JSON):
{
  "clarity": 0-10,
  "confidence": 0-10,
  "relevance": 0-10,
  "fluency": 0-10,
  "energy": 0-10,
  "average": 0-10,
  "verdict": "PASS" | "BORDERLINE" | "FAIL",
  "fillerWordCount": number,
  "flaggedWords": ["word1", "word2"],
  "mtiFlag": true | false,
  "collapseTimestamp": "MM:SS" | null,
  "reportArabic": "Full report in Egyptian colloquial Arabic...",
  "keyFix": "The #1 thing to fix in plain language"
}





PART 4: WHATSAPP BOT SCRIPTS (WORD FOR WORD)


These are the exact messages the bot sends at each stage. Do not rewrite these unless Ahmed approves the new version. Every message follows Egyptian colloquial Arabic rules.

4.1 Bot Intro (IDLE -> STAGE1)
BOT
يلا نبدأ! 👋أنا Gahiz — بحللك صوتك زي ما بيعمل الـ recruiter بالظبط في Teleperformance و Vodafone.التحليل مجاني 100٪. محتاج منك رسالة صوتية واحدة بس.التعليمات:🎤 اضغط على الميكروفون🗣️ اتكلم بالإنجليزي لمدة 60 ثانية عن نفسك📤 ابعت الرسالةالسؤال:"Tell me about yourself. Why do you want to work in a call center?"خد نفس وابدأ. جاهز؟


4.2 After Stage 1 — PASS (Score >= 7.5)
BOT
🎯 Stage 1 Results:Clarity: 8.2 ✅Confidence: 7.8 ✅Fluency: 8.0 ✅Relevance: 7.5 ✅Energy: 8.4 ✅Overall: 7.98 — PASS ✅كويس! طاقتك حلوة وكلامك واضح. بس لاحظت إنك قلت "you know" 3 مرات — ده فيلر word بيضعف الـ impression.عايز تشوف مستواك تحت ضغط أعلى؟جرب Stage 2 — 120 ثانية من سيناريو عميل زعلان.ابعت "Stage 2" لو عايز تكمل 👇


4.3 After Stage 1 — BORDERLINE (Score 5.5-7.4)
BOT
⚠️ Stage 1 Results:Clarity: 6.5Confidence: 6.0Fluency: 5.8Relevance: 7.0Energy: 6.2Overall: 6.3 — BORDERLINE ⚠️أنت قريب، بس لسه مش جاهز.المشكلة الأساسية: بتوقف كتير في النص — recruiters بيحسبوا ده hesitation وبيطلعوا FAIL.محتاج تتدرب على الـ flow. عايز تكمل على Stage 2؟ابعت "Stage 2" 👇


4.4 After Stage 1 — FAIL (Score < 5.5)
BOT
❌ Stage 1 Results:Clarity: 4.5Confidence: 4.0Fluency: 3.8Relevance: 5.0Energy: 4.2Overall: 4.3 — FAIL ❌صريح معاك: لو بعتي الرسالة دي لـ recruiter دلوقتي، هيحذفها في 10 ثواني.مش هجاملك — ده الواقع. بس الحلوة إنك لقيت ده هنا مش في الـ interview الحقيقية.المشكلة الكبيرة: الـ energy والـ fluency محتاجين شغل كبير.عايز تكمل وتشوف امتى بالظبط بتوقع؟ابعت "Stage 2" 👇


4.5 After Stage 3 — Moment of Truth
BOT
🧱 Stage 3 — The Wall Results:Total Collapse at: 2:15Filler Words: 22 units (300% above limit)MTI Accent Flag: 48%Status: UN-HIRABLE ❌ده مش هجوم — ده الحقيقة. 85% من المرشحين بيوقعوا في نفس النقطة.عندك دلوقتي خيارين:🅰️ Option A — مجاناً:اتفرج على بياناتك وجرب مرة تانية. من غير تدريب.🅱️ Option B — 200 جنيه:7 أيام وصول لـ 12 سيناريو nightmare زي الـ interview الحقيقية.تدريب حقيقي. نتائج حقيقية.اختار A ولا B؟ 👇


4.6 After Choosing Option B — Payment
BOT
ممتاز! ✅عشان تفتح الـ 12 simulations:احول 200 جنيه على:💳 Vodafone Cash: 010XXXXXXXXأو💳 Fawry Code: XXXXXXXXXXوابعتلي screenshot الإيصال هنا.بعد ما أتأكد، هبعتلك اللينك فوراً. ⚡


4.7 After Payment Confirmed — Unlock
BOT
✅ تم استلام الدفع!أهلاً في Gahiz Endurance Gym 🏋️اللينك بتاعك:gahiz.eg/dashboard?token=XXXXXXXXXXعندك 7 أيام — 12 سيناريو — مفيش حد شاف السيناريوهات دي غيرك.ابدأ دلوقتي. الـ interview الحقيقية مش بتستنى. 🎯Stay Gahiz. 💪


4.8 Retargeting Message (Day 3 after Option A)
BOT
مازلت فاكرك. 👀يوم 3 من لما قلت 'Option A'.فاكر الـ collapse عند 2:15؟لسه عندك نفس المشكلة.الـ interview الحقيقية مش هتستنى.200 جنيه بس. 7 أيام. 12 سيناريو.لما تكون جاهز، ابعت "جاهز" 👇





PART 5: THE 12 NIGHTMARE SCENARIOS


These are the 12 paid simulation scenarios. Each mirrors a real AI interview interface (HireVue, Talview, Spark Hire style). Questions are company-specific and progressively harder.

Teleperformance Egypt — Level 1 — Digital Screening
Q1
Tell me about yourself and why you want to work at Teleperformance.
Q2
Describe a time you dealt with a frustrated customer. What steps did you take?
Q3
How would you rate your English fluency on a scale of 1-10? Give me an example that justifies your answer.
Q4
What does excellent customer service mean to you?
Q5
Where do you see yourself in 2 years at Teleperformance?


Teleperformance Egypt — Level 2 — Stress Test
Q1
A customer has been on hold for 45 minutes and is now furious. They're threatening to cancel their account. Walk me through exactly what you would say to them.
Q2
You've given a customer incorrect information and they've made a decision based on it that cost them money. How do you handle this call?
Q3
Your system is down. You cannot access the customer's account. The customer needs an urgent resolution. What do you do?
Q4
You're on your 8th hour of shift. You're exhausted. A customer starts verbally abusing you. What's your response?
Q5
Sell me this pen. Make me want to buy it in 60 seconds.


Vodafone UK (Egypt Hub) — Level 1 — Digital Screening
Q1
Why Vodafone? What do you know about our brand and what we stand for?
Q2
Tell me about a time you went above and beyond for a customer.
Q3
Describe your experience with technical support calls. How comfortable are you with troubleshooting?
Q4
How do you handle situations where you don't know the answer to a customer's question?
Q5
What's your expected salary and how did you arrive at that number?


Vodafone UK (Egypt Hub) — Level 2 — Stress Test
Q1
A customer calls to cancel their broadband contract. They've been a customer for 8 years. You have 4 minutes to retain them. Go.
Q2
You're mid-call and the customer starts speaking so fast you can't understand them. They're already frustrated. What do you do?
Q3
A customer insists you promised them a discount on a previous call. You have no record of this. The call is being recorded. How do you handle it?
Q4
Walk me through how you would explain a complex technical outage to a non-technical customer who just wants their internet working.
Q5
Role play: I am a customer who received the wrong device in my order and I've been waiting 3 weeks. I want a refund and compensation. Handle me.


Concentrix Cairo — Level 1 — Digital Screening
Q1
Introduce yourself in English. Focus on your communication skills and why they make you right for this role.
Q2
Describe the most challenging customer interaction you've ever had. What was the outcome?
Q3
Concentrix serves clients across finance, healthcare, and tech. Which sector interests you most and why?
Q4
What's your approach to learning a new product or service quickly?
Q5
Tell me about a time you had to deliver bad news to a customer. How did you frame it?


Amazon BPO Egypt — Level 1 — Digital Screening
Q1
Amazon's leadership principle #1 is Customer Obsession. Give me a real example from your life that demonstrates this principle.
Q2
Describe a time you had to make a decision without all the information you needed. What happened?
Q3
A customer's package shows as delivered but they haven't received it. Walk me through your resolution process.
Q4
Tell me about a time you disagreed with a team decision. How did you handle it?
Q5
Why Amazon? Be specific — not just 'it's a big company.'





PART 6: TARGET CUSTOMER


6.1 Primary Persona: The Candidate
Age
18–35


Location
Cairo or Alexandria


Goal
Land a BPO/call center job at Teleperformance, Vodafone, Concentrix, or Amazon Egypt


Device
Android-first — Samsung A-series, Tecno, Infinix


Connection
4G, not always WiFi


Payment Methods
Vodafone Cash or Fawry. No credit card.


Daily Apps
Facebook (3+ hours), WhatsApp (all day), TikTok, YouTube


6.2 Pain Points
Freezes under pressure during real interviews
Does not know if their English level is good enough
Has never practiced with real BPO-style questions
Fear: accent, pace, filler words, freezing mid-sentence
Has sent WhatsApp voice notes to recruiters and heard nothing back
Does not know why they keep failing — no feedback from recruiters

6.3 Motivation
15,000–25,000 EGP/month = real financial independence
BPO job = global company, professional environment, career path
Wants to beat competition — knows others are also applying
Responds to: direct feedback, visible progress, proof it works




PART 7: ACQUISITION FUNNEL


7.1 Traffic Generation — Facebook Personas
20 aged Facebook personas (not bots — human operators)
Characters: Dina, Kareem, Mostafa, Ahmed, Sarah
Each posts naturally as BPO candidates or employees
Target: Egyptian BPO Facebook groups (50,000–300,000 members each)
Posts drive candidates to the WhatsApp API funnel link
Zero paid advertising in Phase 1 — organic only

7.2 WhatsApp Conversion Flow
Candidate clicks WhatsApp link from Facebook group post
WhatsApp opens with pre-filled message — they hit send
Bot explains Stage 1: Record 60-second intro voice note
Candidate records and sends OGG voice note
Bot converts OGG to MP3 via ffmpeg, sends to AssemblyAI for transcription
AI scores on 5 dimensions, generates Stage 1 AI Report
Bot sends Stage 1 Report + invites candidate to Stage 2
Repeat for Stage 2 (120s) and Stage 3 (240s)
After Stage 3 collapse revealed: bot shows Option A vs Option B (200 EGP)
If Option B: bot sends Vodafone Cash / Fawry payment instructions
Candidate sends screenshot proof of payment
Ahmed verifies and sends UNLOCK command
Bot sends website link with access token for dashboard

7.3 Retargeting Loop
Candidates who choose Option A (free) enter retargeting loop
Bot sends Day 3, Day 5, Day 7 memory messages referencing their collapse score
Message tone: honest and direct — 'We are here when you hit the real one'
After Day 7 with no response: candidate is archived, not deleted




PART 8: WEBSITE STRUCTURE


8.1 Tech Stack
Frontend
Next.js 14 (App Router), TypeScript, Tailwind CSS


Backend
Next.js API Routes, Prisma ORM, PostgreSQL


Auth
NextAuth.js — JWT strategy. Admin = single ADMIN_EMAIL env var


AI — Transcription
AssemblyAI (voice-to-text)


AI — Scoring
OpenAI API (GPT-4) for scoring + feedback generation


Payments
Paymob — Fawry + Vodafone Cash ONLY. Never Stripe or PayPal.


Voice Phase 2
ElevenLabs for AI voice interviewer


Hosting
Vercel. All env vars via Vercel dashboard GUI (no CLI for Ahmed).


WhatsApp Bot
Baileys library on Hugging Face Spaces (Docker). $0 cost.


8.2 Website Pages — Section by Section

/ — Home Page
Section 1: Hero — Logo + Stay Gahiz slogan + two CTAs
CTA 1: 'اختبر إنجليزيتك مجاناً دلوقتي' → WhatsApp link
CTA 2: 'شوف رحلتك للوظيفة' → /journey
Section 2: How It Works — 3 steps (Record → Analyze → Report)
Section 3: The 4-Level Journey preview (visual map)
Section 4: Pricing — Free vs 200 EGP / 7 days
Section 5: Social proof — 3 testimonials with before/after scores
Section 6: FAQ — 5 most common questions
Sticky bottom bar: single WhatsApp CTA (mobile only)

/journey — Customer Journey Page
Section 1: Hero — 'ده اللي بيحصل في الـ interview الحقيقية'
Section 2: Visual timeline of the 4 real hiring levels
Section 3: How Gahiz simulates each level
Section 4: The stamina collapse graph — visual proof
Section 5: Testimonials specific to each company
Section 6: CTA — Start free WhatsApp analysis

/resources — Resources Library
Company guides: Vodafone, Teleperformance, Concentrix, Amazon BPO
Interview tips and vocabulary lists
Stamina training articles
Each resource has a soft CTA to the free WhatsApp analysis

/dashboard — Candidate Dashboard (post-payment)
Header: Days remaining on 7-day access
Section 1: Overall stamina graph over time
Section 2: 12 simulation sessions — locked/unlocked/completed states
Section 3: Score history per session (all 5 dimensions)
Section 4: Key fix recommendations from AI
Section 5: Extend access CTA (200 EGP for another 7 days)

/session — Active Simulation Interface
Mirrors real AI interview link interfaces (HireVue, Talview, Spark Hire style)
Shows question number and company context
4-minute countdown timer
Video recording interface with real-time audio waveform
Submit button + confirm modal

/results/:id — Report Page
Full AI diagnostic report
Score breakdown: all 5 dimensions with color bars
Stamina collapse graph (fluency over time)
Specific feedback with transcript quotes
The #1 fix recommendation (keyFix field from AI)
Upgrade CTA if free tier — next session CTA if paid

/admin — Admin Panel (Ahmed Only)
Candidate list: phone, state, date, score
Search by phone number
Manual payment verify + UNLOCK button
Platform stats: daily sessions, conversions, revenue today
Access: ADMIN_EMAIL env var only — redirect all others to /
⚠ The /booking coached session page is Phase 3 only. Do not build in Phase 1.

8.3 Environment Variables
DATABASE_URL — PostgreSQL connection (Supabase or Vercel Postgres)
NEXTAUTH_SECRET — Random 32-char string
NEXTAUTH_URL — https://gahiz.eg
ADMIN_EMAIL — Ahmed's email (sole admin)
OPENAI_API_KEY — Scoring and feedback generation
ASSEMBLYAI_API_KEY — Voice transcription
PAYMOB_API_KEY — Paymob authentication
PAYMOB_INTEGRATION_ID — Fawry/Vodafone Cash integration
PAYMOB_IFRAME_ID — Hosted payment page
PAYMOB_HMAC_SECRET — Webhook HMAC verification
WHATSAPP_BOT_SECRET — Internal bot-to-web API calls




PART 9: WHATSAPP BOT (TECHNICAL)


9.1 Tech Stack
Library: Baileys (reverse-engineers WhatsApp Web — $0 cost)
Hosting: Hugging Face Spaces via Docker (stays awake, $0)
Database: Supabase Free Tier (500MB — stores 50,000+ candidate records)
Transcription: AssemblyAI or Groq Whisper-large-v3
AI Scoring: OpenAI GPT-4 or Groq Llama-3-70b
Audio Conversion: ffmpeg (installed on server)
⚠ Baileys can get WhatsApp number banned. Use dedicated SIM, not personal number.

9.2 Candidate State Machine
IDLE
No active session. Waiting for trigger message.


STAGE1
Recording 60-second intro voice note.


STAGE2
Recording 120-second de-escalation scenario.


STAGE3
Recording 240-second marathon — the Wall.


MOMENT_OF_TRUTH
Collapse revealed. Showing Option A vs Option B.


AWAITING_PAYMENT
Candidate chose Option B. Waiting for payment screenshot.


UNLOCKED
Payment verified. Website access token sent.


RETARGETING
Candidate chose Option A. Receiving memory messages.


9.3 Admin Commands
UNLOCK 201XXXXXXXXX
Manually unlock candidate after payment verification


STATUS 201XXXXXXXXX
Check candidate's current state in the flow


STATS
Show today's numbers: sessions started, completed, converted, revenue


9.4 Failure State Handling
If voice note fails to transcribe:
BOT
معلش، الصوت مش واضح كفاية. ممكن تعيد التسجيل في مكان أهدى؟ 🎤


If candidate goes silent after Stage 1 (24 hours):
BOT
لسه عندك اللينك. Stage 2 لو عايز تكمل، ابعت 'Stage 2'. لو مش مستعد دلوقتي، تمام. إحنا هنا. 👋


If payment screenshot is unclear:
BOT
الـ screenshot مش واضح. ممكن تبعت صورة تانية فيها الرقم والمبلغ والتاريخ؟ ✅





PART 10: INVESTOR POSITIONING (PROJECT GAZE)


10.1 The Triple-Win SaaS Model
Candidate Gets:
2-minute assessment with real recruiter-calibrated feedback
Risk mitigation — knows weak points before the real interview
Ban prevention — avoids blacklisting by practicing first

Recruiter Gets:
Link distribution tools (Phase 3)
Noise cancellation — pre-screened candidates only
Verified shortlist — only B2+ candidates sent

Gahiz (Admin) Gets:
Platform authority in the Egyptian BPO hiring ecosystem
Member loyalty — candidates return for every application cycle
Community data — what questions, which companies, failure patterns

10.2 Market Size
200,000 annual BPO applicants in Egypt
85% (170,000) identified as B1 Near-Miss — at-risk of failing
Target: 10 daily sales = 3,650 sales/year = 730,000 EGP/year at launch
6-month projection: 243,000 EGP cumulative revenue

10.3 Competitive Moat
Global AI (Google, Final Round)
Too polite, corporate, locks local context. Not built for Egyptian BPO.


Regional B2B Platforms
Sell training to HR departments. Lose candidate trust.


Gahiz
200 EGP. Understands urgency. Understands Egyptian market. Candidate-first.


10.4 Financial Targets
Phase 1 Target: 10 daily sales
Month 1 Revenue: 10 x 200 EGP x 30 days = 60,000 EGP
Month 6 Revenue: scaling to 210,000 EGP/month
Infrastructure cost: $0 (zero-budget Phase 1 stack)




PART 11: MASTER OPERATING RULES


11.1 Always
Ahmed is non-technical. Explain everything in plain English.
Mobile first. Design at 390px. Android baseline (Samsung A-series).
RTL for all Arabic-language screens. dir=rtl and lang=ar always.
Zero placeholder content. No Lorem Ipsum. No fake prices.
Paymob + Fawry + Vodafone Cash ONLY for payments.
Egyptian colloquial Arabic for candidates. Never MSA.
Score labels always in English: Clarity, Confidence, Fluency, Energy, Relevance.
Deploy via Vercel dashboard GUI only. No CLI assumed.

11.2 Never
Suggest Stripe, PayPal, or non-Egyptian payment methods.
Use corporate jargon in candidate-facing copy.
Make false promises (100% success guaranteed).
Use MSA (Modern Standard Arabic) in candidate messages.
Price in USD for B2C Egyptian audience.
Use personal WhatsApp number for the bot — dedicated SIM only.
Mention coached sessions (350 EGP) in Phase 1 — that is Phase 3.
Build /booking page in Phase 1.

11.3 Critical Language Rules
Candidate greeting
Egyptian colloquial: يلا نبدأ — never عزيزي or تحية طيبة


Address style
أنت (informal) — never حضرتك


Verdict format
English label + Arabic description: PASS — عدت وأنت جاهز!


Price format
200 EGP or 200 جنيه — never $200 or USD


Company names
Always English: Teleperformance, Vodafone, Concentrix, Amazon
